title: [codex] Validate sliding-window post-quant evaluation on 1xH100 proxy
author: Kevxn97
body:
## Summary

This draft PR captures my current best validated parameter-golf attempt so far: a sliding-window post-quant evaluation variant built on top of the baseline training path.

The core idea is simple: the baseline validation path scores many tokens with very limited left context because validation is chunked into non-overlapping windows. This PR adds a sliding-window evaluation mode so that each scored token sees near-max context at evaluation time while keeping training behavior unchanged.

I am using this as a serious proxy line while searching for stronger architectures. My current workflow is to use 1xGPU proxy runs to rank ideas by final post-quantized performance, then only spend larger multi-GPU budget on the most credible candidates.

## Why this matters

For parameter golf, the metric that matters is not just training loss or even pre-quant validation, but the final post-quantized score after the exact export path. This PR focuses on improving that part of the pipeline directly and serves as a validated baseline for future architecture search.

## Current validated result

Best validated proxy result on my side so far:

- Hardware: RunPod 1xH100 proxy run
- Branch/commit lineage: `codex/sliding-window-eval-v1`
- Exact printed metric: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb: 1.32756718`

This is not presented as a final official leaderboard submission. It is my current best validated attempt and a stable reference point for future work.

## Main changes

- add sliding-window validation support
- add configuration knobs for evaluation stride / batching
- keep training path otherwise close to baseline
- document local + RunPod workflow for reproducibility
- record validated experiment details in the repo tracker

## Validation

I validated the approach with a real proxy run and recorded the final exact post-quant metric above. The main purpose of this PR is to provide a credible, reproducible baseline and demonstrate an active, evidence-driven workflow for continued submission development.

## Notes

This PR is intentionally marked as draft. I am still using it as a base for further experiments and compute-efficient filtering before opening a stronger final submission.
